name: Brex API
what_it_does: Brex is a financial platform for startups and growing companies offering corporate cards, expense management, business accounts, and vendor payments. Its API provides programmatic access to card management, transactions, expense submissions, vendor payments, and spend limit controls.
use_cases: Automated expense categorization and receipt matching
Corporate card issuance and limit management for employees
Expense report generation and accounting sync
not_for: Small businesses below Brex's eligibility requirements
best_when: An agent needs to manage corporate card spend, automate expense workflows, or issue/manage cards programmatically for a startup or growing company on Brex.
avoid_when: The company uses Ramp, Divvy, or another expense platform, or is not eligible for Brex (typically requires VC backing or significant revenue).

auth.notes: OAuth2 for user-authorized access with granular scopes (transactions.readonly, cards.write, payments.write, etc.). Machine-to-machine auth available via client credentials grant for internal integrations. API tokens can be generated directly in the Brex dashboard for simpler use cases.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No monthly fee for Brex cards or API access. API is free for Brex customers.
pricing.paid_tiers: Brex Premium $12/user/mo for enhanced features. No API-specific pricing.
pricing.notes: Brex makes money on interchange fees from card transactions. API access is included free for all Brex customers. No usage-based API pricing.

performance.rate_limits: Specific rate limits not publicly documented; standard RESTful throttling with 429 responses applies.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Errors returned as standard HTTP status codes with JSON body containing error and message fields. Error descriptions are generally clear for validation failures. Some edge cases return generic messages. OpenAPI spec is available which helps agents validate requests before sending.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Idempotency-Key header supported on payment and card creation endpoints. Not universally available across all write endpoints. Recommended for payment initiation to prevent duplicate disbursements.
known_agent_gotchas: Brex eligibility requirements mean not all businesses can access the platform — API is only useful if the company is already a Brex customer
Card limits and spend controls may need human approval for changes above certain thresholds
Payment status is asynchronous — ACH and wire transfers are not immediate; agents must poll or use webhooks
Transaction data for card purchases may have a delay before appearing in API
OAuth token scopes must be carefully configured — overly broad scopes are a security risk on corporate finance APIs
Webhook payload verification is required — HMAC signing must be validated
Some operations (issuing new cards) may trigger compliance checks that delay or reject the operation

score_rationale: Brex API is developer-friendly with a published OpenAPI spec, OAuth2 with granular scopes, cursor pagination, and idempotency on key payment endpoints. The free API access for Brex customers removes pricing friction. The main limitations are US-only coverage, eligibility requirements restricting who can use Brex, asynchronous payment processing, and some compliance-triggered delays on card operations. A solid choice for startup spend management automation when the company is already on Brex.

security_score_components.security_notes: Corporate spend management API. OAuth2 with granular scopes (expenses, payments, cards). Financial data — use minimal scopes. Webhook signatures for event validation.
